summary: Mitoxantrone is not supported by evidence as an effective alternative to combination chemotherapy in high-risk metastatic breast cancer; current standards rely on synergistic multi-drug regimens rather than single-agent therapy. There are no studies demonstrating superior effectiveness of first-line single-agent mitoxantrone compared to combination therapies.
